channel.title: Transcripts: Prof G Pod (Scott Galloway)
channel.description: YouTube video transcripts from Prof G Pod (Scott Galloway)

item.title: “I’m…very afraid of a prolonged war”
item.description: Trump once campaigned on ending “forever wars,” but new military action against Iran suggests a major shift. Scott Galloway and Jessica Tarlov discuss, on Raging Moderates.
item.content:encoded:
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/2NRjDKNWaxc

Transcript

President Trump is calling it a massive and ongoing targeting of Iran's nuclear program, missile industry, navy, and even signaling support for regime change. Are we about to enter a forever war with boots on the ground? >> The thing that feels different about this from other actions that President Trump has taken in both the first term and the second term is that he actually gave a speech where he said there may be American casualties. and he campaigned both times on being the peace president. He loves to take a victory lap on being

right about Iraq and Afghanistan. And this was something that was really uniting for both the right and the left that people don't want to be in these wars anymore. And you're seeing veterans from across the political spectrum who are about my age who signed up after 9/11 to go and risk their lives saying, "What's the deal here? Haven't we seen this movie before?" So I am conflicted at the moment but very afraid of the prolonged war. >> The Straits of Hormuz have not been under a constant level of havoc or or or siege. You haven't seen missiles flying into Israel from Hezbollah. You haven't

seen all this sort of insecurity and quite frankly terror. Uh Syria has been decapitated. Russia is no longer a threat there because they've lost their proxy in Syria. The hands of Iran have basically been cut off. And I would argue that uh this is, if you will, a bit of a blinking green light that we should finish the job.

item.title: “The Trump administration is in retreat”
item.description: Donald Trump addressed the nation with his rationale for bombing Iran, but Jessica Tarlov isn’t buying it — on Raging Moderates.

item.title: Trump LOSES CONTROL as UNLAWFUL War in Iran ERUPTS
item.description:
Scott Galloway and Jessica Tarlov are joined by retired U.S. Navy Admiral James Stavridis to break down a rapidly escalating crisis in the Middle East. Following U.S. and Israeli strikes that killed Iran’s Supreme Leader, Tehran has formed a transitional council and is vowing retaliation. U.S. troops are dead. Missiles are flying between Iran, Israel, and American bases across the Gulf. The Strait of Hormuz is under threat, the price of oil is spiking, and global markets are on edge. Is there an off-ramp — or are we at the start of a wider war? What does regime decapitation actually accomplish? How might Iran respond — cyberattacks, proxies, broader regional escalation? And what does this mean for oil prices, the global economy, China, and President Trump’s legacy?
#scottgalloway #jessicatarlov #trump #iran #war #ayatollahalikhamenei #supremeleader #israel #netanyahu #marcorubio #petehegseth #nuclearweapons #tehran
Follow Jessica Tarlov, @JessicaTarlov Follow Prof G, @TheProfGPod Follow Raging Moderates, @RagingModerates
Subscribe to our YouTube Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@RagingModerates

item.title: “Early stages of a very long war?”
item.description: Admiral James Stavridis joins Scott Galloway and Jessica Tarlov to discuss potential outcomes for the current conflict in Iran, on Raging Moderates.
item.content:encoded:
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/4HeSv65TbJg

Transcript

Admiral, from a military standpoint, is there a clear offramp here, or are we looking at the very early stages of a longer war in the Middle East? >> I'll give you three scenarios very quickly. First one is the happy scenario. This would be where the people of Iran rise up and overthrow the revolutionary guard. I think sadly that's a 30% chance at best. The other 70% breaks into two scenarios. one, you could kind of think of it as Iran 3.0 that includes some remnants of this

government, but uh that behaves in a more moderate, sensible fashion. And then there's kind of the dark end of the spectrum in that 70% tunch, and that's that team moola Ayatollah toughs it out, takes the bombing, no boots on the ground are coming, and they repress the populace even worse. So there's a snapshot of the future as I see it.
